topic: Change-driven c7 re-review: 3-6 vs 3-9 µGal field inconsistency from partial c6 patch
change_summary: detail_evidence_length grew from 2119 to 2704 (+27.6%) since c6 (2026-05-11T06:40). Drift detected via monitor/integrity/drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-24T05:42:10Z. obe_skipped=1 (WIN-019 already re-reviewed at 2026-05-24T14:55). The growth reflects: (a) c6 hole #1 partially applied — '3–6 µGal' widened to '3–9 µGal' in detail_evidence and tldr_evidence ONLY; finding and detail_verdict_text were not updated; (b) an alternative-anchor citation (Mansinha et al. 2001 J. Geodet. Soc. Japan, DOI 10.11366/sokuchi1954.47.387) was added to detail_evidence alongside the c6 ISS-1944 annotation of the dome's broken Van Camp 2001 DOI.
our_argument_summary: Membach SG 1999 null is cited by the dome as confirming, but applying the dome's own κ = 1.677 nT/µGal (WIN-012) to the documented 1999 European-eclipse magnetic perturbation (multi-site 5–15 nT envelope per Strestík 2001, Hvožďara & Prigancová 2002, Curto et al. 2006) predicts a contact-phase Δg of order 3–9 µGal — well above the SG noise floor (0.01–0.1 µGal). The SG recorded zero. Verdict: Refuted by Data. Defense carve-outs are addressed structurally in Part 6.14 (κ Coupling Cluster) as a vise: every carve-out exempting 1999 from κ also destroys PRED-TIER-3's 10–13 µGal August 2026 derivation.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: monitor.py contains no Membach domain and no SG-null verification logic for WIN-013. Domain 7 tautologically validates κ=1.67 against itself (KAPPA hardcoded; kappa_obs = 10.9/6.5 = 1.677 hardcoded). No data fetch from any SG archive or BGS/INTERMAGNET endpoint.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Hardcoded identity check for κ in monitor.py Domain 7; no monitor.py code targets WIN-013 specifically.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Dome live-site escape clauses (a) Chebychev low-pass attenuates contact-phase transients before Level-3 release; (b) measurements were not optimized for C1/C4 timing correlation. Both rebutted structurally in WIN-012 / Part 6.14.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new. Domain #7 vs dashboard Domain 5 numbering mismatch (ISS-1946) remains open and out of scope; the c6 finding on this still applies.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py has no Membach domain, no Van Camp data fetch, no SG-null verification. Tag unchanged from c6.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics does not predict a non-zero eclipse SG signal at Membach in 1999. The dome makes its own non-standard prediction via κ. Tag unchanged from c6.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The Membach SG eclipse measurement was publicly indexed for ~25 years before the dome cited it. Tag unchanged from c6.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: 0.0 µGal is an adopted observation, not derived from dome geometry. Tag unchanged from c6.

holes_found.description: Partial-patch field inconsistency: the c6 moderate finding (widening '3–6 µGal' → '3–9 µGal' to match the 5–15 nT ΔB envelope through κ = 1.677 nT/µGal) was applied to detail_evidence and tldr_evidence only. The two HEADLINE fields — `finding` (summary-table row, the first text a reader encounters) and `detail_verdict_text` (the verdict box rendered above the expanded detail) — still say '3–6 µGal contact-phase pulse' / 'gravity pulse of order 3–6 µGal'. Result: WIN-013 now carries two contradictory contact-phase predictions across its own fields. Finding/verdict say 3–6 µGal (implying ΔB = 5–10 nT); detail_evidence and tldr_evidence say 3–9 µGal (consistent with the explicitly cited 5–15 nT ΔB range). Cross-WIN: WIN-014 (China SG null) uses '3–9 µGal' uniformly across finding, detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, and tldr_evidence for the parallel κ-coupling argument with the same ΔB envelope. A dome defender reading both WINs side-by-side sees: 'Same κ. Same ΔB envelope (5–15 nT). Same physics. WIN-013 headline says 3–6 µGal, WIN-014 headline says 3–9 µGal. Which range does your model commit to?' Math: 5 / 1.677 = 2.98 µGal, 10 / 1.677 = 5.96 µGal, 15 / 1.677 = 8.94 µGal — so 3–6 µGal implies a 5–10 nT range, and 3–9 µGal implies the full 5–15 nT range that the detail prose actually cites. The refutation force is unchanged (3 µGal vs 6 vs 9 are all 30–900× above the 0.01–0.1 µGal noise floor), but the appearance of unpatched arithmetic is the kind of slip a calculator-armed defender finds in 30 seconds — same critique pattern as the c6 hole, now in different fields.
holes_found.recommendation: Update two strings to '3–9 µGal' so all five field-text loci agree. (a) In `finding` change 'predicts a 3–6 µGal contact-phase pulse' to 'predicts a 3–9 µGal contact-phase pulse' (matches WIN-014 finding tone). (b) In `detail_verdict_text` change 'predicts a contact-phase gravity pulse of order 3–6 µGal' to 'predicts a contact-phase gravity pulse of order 3–9 µGal'. After the swap, all of finding / detail_evidence / detail_verdict_text / tldr_evidence carry '3–9 µGal' and the explicitly cited 5–15 nT envelope (in detail_evidence) is arithmetically consistent with the prediction in every field. Decider can self-apply; no verdict shift, no citation change.

stronger_arguments.description: Pre-empt the C1/C4-transient-amplitude defense. A dome defender could argue the 5–15 nT envelope describes the TOTALITY magnetic perturbation, but κ couples specifically to C1/C4 onset/release transients. Multi-site eclipse-magnetic literature reports C1/C4 transient amplitudes that are roughly 30–60% of the totality envelope (this varies by station and by the magnetic component examined; the literature is heterogeneous on whether the 'contact-phase' signal is the eclipse onset transient itself or the broader totality envelope, which the dome's monitor.py does not disambiguate). At 30% scaling, ΔB(C1/C4) = 1.5–4.5 nT through κ predicts Δg = 0.9–2.7 µGal — still 9–270× above the SG noise floor, so the refutation survives, but the prediction's lower bound now approaches the floor rather than crushing it. Adding one short clause in detail_evidence acknowledging the C1/C4-only carve-out and showing the math still works (e.g., '… even if κ were restricted to C1/C4 contact transients at ~30% of the totality envelope, ΔB(C1/C4) ≈ 1.5–4.5 nT predicts Δg ≈ 0.9–2.7 µGal — still ~10× to ~270× above the SG noise floor.') preempts this defense without inflating the WIN. The cluster section (Part 6.14) is the natural home for the version that applies across WIN-013/014/058b/PRED-TIER-3.
sources: Hvožďara & Prigancová 2002 (cited in current detail_evidence; resolves the totality envelope and partially documents C1/C4 onset transients)
Crossley, Hinderer & Riccardi 2013 (already cited in WIN-014 for SG noise floor)
Curto et al. 2006 (cited in current detail_evidence; describes time-evolution of eclipse magnetic signature)

stronger_arguments.description: Surface the Mansinha alt-anchor citation (DOI 10.11366/sokuchi1954.47.387) more prominently. The c5 review suggested Van Camp 2006 (DOI 10.1029/2006JB004405) as an alternative anchor; somewhere between c6 and now, Mansinha et al. 2001 (J. Geodet. Soc. Japan) was added instead — a different (and valid) choice. Mansinha covers the multi-station SG null directly (Membach + Uccles + Strasbourg + Vienna), which is closer to what WIN-013 actually claims than Van Camp 2006's later hydrogeology work. The current prose puts the dome's broken DOI first ('The dome cites… but… does not resolve') and only then names the alternative anchor. A defender pivoting from 'your DOI is broken' to 'you can't find a real source' is now answered by the Mansinha citation — but a reader who skims the first sentence may miss it. Consider rewording the second sentence of paragraph 2 to lead with Mansinha as the anchor rather than the broken Van Camp DOI as the dome's chosen reference; this is style, not a correctness issue.

kernel_of_truth.description: The Membach SG null is a real measurement and the multi-station 1999-eclipse SG campaign at Membach, Uccles, Strasbourg, and Vienna is well-documented (data/wins.json:013/detail_evidence cites Mansinha, Ducarme, Hinderer, Meurers & van Ruymbeke 2001, J. Geodet. Soc. Japan DOI 10.11366/sokuchi1954.47.387 as the verified anchor; this replaces the dome's broken DOI 10.1023/A:1013940928637 — a Springer/Kluwer prefix that cannot belong to an Elsevier journal, per monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json:ISS-1944). The dome's instinct to cite a multi-station 1999 SG null as empirical anchor for any claim about eclipse-induced gravity behavior is reasonable; the null is real, the precision floor (0.01–0.1 µGal) is real, and the team at the Royal Observatory of Belgium is real.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The null itself is not in dispute. What the dome calls 'WIN' is the framing that null confirms the dome model. That framing only holds if no published dome mechanism predicts a non-null at Membach in 1999. The dome's own κ = 1.677 nT/µGal (data/wins.json:012; monitor.py Domain 7) is exactly such a mechanism — κ × ΔB = Δg, and the ΔB for 1999 multi-site totality is 5–15 nT (data/wins.json:013/detail_evidence cites Strestík 2001 ΔY≈+10 / ΔX≈−5 nT and Hvožďara & Prigancová 2002 ΔX=−5 to −8 / ΔY=+12 to +15 nT). Through κ, ΔB predicts Δg = 3–9 µGal at Membach (verified arithmetically: 5/1.677 = 2.98, 15/1.677 = 8.94). The SG measured zero at a 0.01–0.1 µGal floor — that is 30× to 900× below the κ-predicted contact-phase pulse. The dome cannot simultaneously hold (a) κ as a published constant (PRED-TIER-3 needs κ to derive its 10–13 µGal August 2026 prediction; see data/wins.json:013/detail_evidence cross-reference to Part 6.14) and (b) the 1999 SG null as confirming. The verdict 'Refuted by Data' (data/wins.json:013/verdict) is robust independent of the current 3–6 vs 3–9 µGal field-text inconsistency: any number in the 3–9 µGal range refutes a 0 µGal measurement at 0.01–0.1 µGal precision.

advocate_mode.best_defense: The κ = 1.677 nT/µGal coupling describes the EVENT-DURATION (totality) magnetic-to-gravity transfer, not the contact-phase transients C1/C4 specifically. Multi-site eclipse-magnetic literature reports that C1/C4 onset/release transients are typically 30–60% of the totality envelope; the dome's WIN claim is properly read as 'no anomalous C1/C4 transient' rather than 'no κ-coupled gravity at any time during the eclipse.' At 30% scaling, ΔB(C1/C4) for 1999 Membach is 1.5–4.5 nT, predicting Δg = 0.9–2.7 µGal — still detectable above the 0.01–0.1 µGal floor but no longer the 'crushing' refutation the review describes; the SG could plausibly have detected a 0.9 µGal transient and rejected it as instrumental noise rather than a κ-coupled signal. Moreover, the Chebychev low-pass filter the dome cites in its escape clauses is operationally applied at SG facilities before Level-3 release — it preferentially attenuates short-duration transients precisely in the C1/C4 timescale. The review's '0 µGal measured / 3–9 µGal predicted' framing thus elides two real engineering effects, neither of which the dome invented post-hoc: (1) κ is not necessarily an instantaneous-transfer constant but may represent an integrated-totality effect; (2) the Chebychev filter is part of the standard SG data-reduction pipeline and is not a dome-specific carve-out.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Add one or two sentences to detail_evidence explicitly engaging the C1/C4-transient-amplitude defense and the Chebychev filter as TWO related-but-distinct objections. The cluster section (Part 6.14) may be the better venue if it's shared across WIN-013/014/058b/PRED-TIER-3. Concretely: '... even granting a coverage-style restriction of κ to C1/C4 contact transients (commonly reported at 30–60% of totality envelope amplitude in the eclipse-magnetic literature), ΔB(C1/C4) = 1.5–9 nT through κ still predicts Δg = 0.9–5.4 µGal at Membach — 9× to 540× above the SG noise floor. The Chebychev filter operates with a corner at ~0.1 Hz (timescales ≲10 s), passing the multi-minute C1/C4 transient nearly unattenuated. Both engineering carve-outs preserve a κ-predicted signal large enough for the SG to detect; the network-wide null still refutes.' This converts a 'survives=3' rhetorical hole into a 'survives=1' explicit-dismissal hole.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-013 (Membach 1999) and WIN-014 (China 2009) apply the same κ-coupling argument across two eclipses. WIN-014 uses '3–9 µGal' uniformly across finding, detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, and tldr_evidence. WIN-013 mixes '3–6 µGal' (finding, detail_verdict_text) and '3–9 µGal' (detail_evidence, tldr_evidence). Same physics; same κ; same multi-site 5–15 nT envelope per the same eclipse-magnetic literature (Strestík, Hvožďara, Curto for 1999; Kim & Chang for 2009 — both windows of the dome's own 39-eclipse WIN-068 ensemble). The mismatch is purely a partial-patch artifact from c6 and does not change either WIN's verdict, but a defender comparing the two WINs side-by-side has a free 'your arithmetic is inconsistent across your own κ-cluster WINs' rebuttal. Hole #1 above prescribes the fix. WIN-012 (κ definition itself) is consistent — it does not state a specific Δg prediction. WIN-068 (39-eclipse ensemble) is consistent — it states the 5–15 nT envelope without claiming a specific µGal prediction.

claims_checked: κ = 1.677 nT/µGal (dome-published, from 10.9 nT / 6.5 µGal in monitor.py Domain 7)
ΔB envelope = 5–15 nT for 1999 multi-site totality (Strestík 2001, Hvožďara & Prigancová 2002, Curto 2006)
Δg = ΔB / κ at the two stated range boundaries
Δg predictions relative to SG noise floor (0.01–0.1 µGal)
quantitative_verification.computations: Direct division: 5 / 1.677 = 2.982 µGal (rounds to 3); 10 / 1.677 = 5.963 µGal (rounds to 6); 15 / 1.677 = 8.945 µGal (rounds to 9). The '3–6 µGal' string in finding and detail_verdict_text corresponds arithmetically to a 5–10 nT ΔB range. The '3–9 µGal' string in detail_evidence and tldr_evidence corresponds arithmetically to the full 5–15 nT ΔB range that detail_evidence explicitly cites from the literature. Both endpoints in the 3–9 µGal range exceed the 0.1 µGal upper-bound SG noise floor by 30× (lower) to 90× (upper); even 0.9 µGal (C1/C4 transient at 30% scaling of the 5 nT lower bound) exceeds the 0.1 µGal floor by 9×.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Internal to WIN-013: the '3–6 µGal' figure in finding and detail_verdict_text does not match the 5–15 nT envelope the prose cites; it matches a narrower 5–10 nT envelope that no longer appears in the WIN's own detail_evidence after the c6 widening. This is the partial-patch hole in `holes_found[0]`. No external-citation discrepancies found in this run.

citations_verified: Mansinha, Ducarme, Hinderer, Meurers & van Ruymbeke 2001 — J. Geodet. Soc. Japan 47(1) 387 — DOI 10.11366/sokuchi1954.47.387, URL https://www.jstage.jst.go.jp/article/sokuchi1954/47/1/47_1_387/_article — URL format matches J-STAGE convention for the Japanese-language geodetic journal; not externally fetched this run but the DOI prefix (10.11366) is the correct J-STAGE prefix for that journal. Carry-over verification from the implicit acceptance in commits between c6 and the 2026-05-21 disaster recovery.
citations_failed: Van Camp et al. 2001 DOI 10.1023/A:1013940928637 — dome's printed citation; correctly annotated in detail_evidence as unverifiable (prefix 10.1023 is Springer/Kluwer, inconsistent with Phys. Earth Planet. Inter. which is Elsevier prefix 10.1016). ISS-1944 is closed. Annotation is the right call — preserves reader access to the dome's source intent while flagging the resolution failure.
citations_unchecked: Strestík 2001 (Springer chapter, URL https://link.springer.com/chapter/10.1007/978-94-010-0800-6_55) — confirmed resolving by c6; not re-fetched this run.

Hvožďara & Prigancová 2002 — DOI 10.1029/2002JA009260 — flagged as 'ResearchGate-only' by c5 (ISS-1947 still open); not re-validated this run. The 5–15 nT range survives this single-citation uncertainty because Strestík and Curto independently anchor the same envelope.
Curto et al. 2006 — DOI 10.1029/2005JA011499 — not re-validated this run; URL pattern is standard AGU JGR; deferred to next deep-attack cycle (carry-over from c6).

deeper_analysis:
Change-driven c7 review of WIN-013, triggered by drift-audit head pick (detail_evidence_length +27.6% since c6). The drift is composed of two distinct sub-edits that happened between c6 (2026-05-11T06:40) and the disaster-recovery commit 45dbdc6 (2026-05-24T01:06:40): (i) the c6 hole #1 fix — 3–6 → 3–9 µGal — partially applied to detail_evidence and tldr_evidence only; (ii) the c5 alternative-anchor suggestion implemented in a different form than the c5 proposal — Mansinha et al. 2001 J. Geodet. Soc. Japan added alongside (not replacing) the dome's broken Van Camp 2001 DOI annotation.

The (ii) edit is unambiguously an improvement: it gives readers a verified anchor for the underlying multi-station SG null, hardens the WIN against a 'your source citation is broken' pivot from the dome, and is internally consistent with the verdict.

The (i) edit created the moderate hole flagged above. The partial application is mechanically the same kind of slip the c5 reviewer caught (then in tldr_evidence vs detail_evidence; now in finding/detail_verdict_text vs detail_evidence/tldr_evidence). Recommendation is two narrow string replacements (see hole #1 recommendation); decider can self-apply with no verdict shift.

Advocate-mode lens identified a defense_survives=3 vulnerability: the C1/C4-transient amplitude scaling + Chebychev-filter pair of objections. The refutation survives both objections at any plausible scaling, but the current detail_evidence treats them gestureally rather than walking through the arithmetic. A two-sentence preemption (proposed in advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation) converts this from a survives=3 hole to a survives=1 dismissal. This recommendation is stronger than the moderate finding (which is purely a string-fix) but lower-priority because the verdict already holds and the cluster section may be the more natural home for the preemption.

Cross-WIN lens identified the same field-inconsistency in WIN-013 specifically by comparison against WIN-014's uniform 3–9 µGal treatment. WIN-012 and WIN-068 are compatible.

Quantitative lens verified the ΔB/κ arithmetic; no math errors in detail_evidence or tldr_evidence; the 3–6 µGal in finding and detail_verdict_text is internally arithmetically consistent (with an implicit 5–10 nT) but inconsistent with the explicit 5–15 nT envelope the rest of the WIN cites.

Non-scope reminders (carried from c6, still open): ISS-1946 (Domain #7 vs Domain 5 dashboard numbering) — current prose still says 'monitor.py Domain #7'; ISS-1947 (Hvožďara ResearchGate-only). Neither is re-flagged here.

Verdict assessment: current_verdict_holds=true. confidence: 0.78 (down from c6's 0.82 — the partial-patch artifact costs ~0.04). recommended_action: minor_edit.
summary_for_decider:
WIN-013 c7 — change-driven, drift-audit head pick (after OBE-skipping WIN-019 which was re-reviewed earlier today).

ONE MODERATE HOLE: partial-patch field inconsistency. The c6 hole #1 fix ('3–6 µGal' → '3–9 µGal' to match the 5–15 nT envelope through κ) was applied to detail_evidence and tldr_evidence only. The summary-table `finding` and the verdict-box `detail_verdict_text` still say '3–6 µGal'. WIN-014 (parallel κ-cluster member) uses '3–9 µGal' uniformly across all its fields — a defender comparing the two WINs side-by-side has a free 'your arithmetic is inconsistent across your own cluster' rebuttal. Fix is two narrow string replacements (specified in holes_found[0].recommendation); no verdict shift, no citation change. Decider can self-apply.

ONE STRONGER-ARGUMENT SUGGESTION (advocate-mode survives=3): pre-empt the C1/C4-transient-amplitude + Chebychev-filter defense in two sentences. Math walks through that the refutation survives at any plausible scaling. May be a better fit in the cluster section (Part 6.14) than in WIN-013 detail_evidence, since the same preemption helps WIN-014, WIN-058b, and PRED-TIER-3. Lower priority than the moderate hole — verdict already holds.

KERNEL-OF-TRUTH UPDATE: the Mansinha et al. 2001 alt-anchor citation (added between c6 and 2026-05-21 disaster recovery) replaces the c5 reviewer's Van Camp 2006 suggestion with a directly-relevant multi-station 1999 SG paper. Improvement. Worth a one-sentence reword to lead with Mansinha rather than the broken Van Camp DOI; style only, optional.

CARRY-OVER STILL OPEN (not re-flagged this run): ISS-1946 (Domain #7 vs Domain 5 dashboard) — same status as c6. ISS-1947 (Hvožďara ResearchGate-only) — same. Defer.
